notes: Demo mode uses offline fixtures for all providers.

datasetMetadata.productionSafetyStatement: NOT PRODUCTION SAFE: this snapshot contains demo fixtures, approximate geometries, and derived scenario records with no exact external matches.
datasetMetadata.summary: Operational-looking provider names and event claims were reclassified so synthetic fixtures and derived scenario records cannot be mistaken for verified real-world events.
datasetMetadata.providerIdentityPolicy: Original provider names are preserved only as legacyProvider fields. Active provider fields are renamed to fixture identifiers when payload lineage is synthetic or seeded.

regionTaggingPolicy.method: geometry intersection against coarse built-in polygons
regionTaggingPolicy.note: Region assignments are approximate because the built-in regions use simplified polygons that overlap. Recomputed regionIds preserve all geometric overlaps and mark them as approximate.

confidenceModel.name: integrity_repair_claim_confidence_v1
confidenceModel.semanticMeaning: Confidence estimates the defensibility of a real-world claim after provenance and external-verification penalties, not internal fixture coherence alone.

controlledVocabulary.thresholdRule: Use only when multi-signal evidence suggests a strike-like scenario but no authoritative confirmation exists.
controlledVocabulary.evidenceRule: Must not be used as verified real-world confirmation; synthetic lineage or missing exact external match keeps recordStatus below verified_real.

controlledVocabulary.thresholdRule: Use for restriction-like records that remain useful structurally but are not validated as authoritative live restrictions.
controlledVocabulary.evidenceRule: Synthetic or seeded restriction payloads must remain synthetic_demo and carry advisory wording.

controlledVocabulary.thresholdRule: Use for clustered traffic-pattern changes without claiming cause certainty.
controlledVocabulary.evidenceRule: Do not describe sparse or interrupted coverage as transponder loss unless independently supported.

controlledVocabulary.thresholdRule: Use for grouped thermal observations without attributing cause.
controlledVocabulary.evidenceRule: Thermal-only observations cannot imply strike confirmation by themselves.

controlledVocabulary.thresholdRule: Use when a report remains weakly sourced or unmatched externally.
controlledVocabulary.evidenceRule: Single-source or no-match claims stay explicitly unverified.
